channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: China tests nuclear-capable missile in South Pacific; Australia condemns move amid regional security tensions
item.description: China has announced plans to test-fire a nuclear-capable intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) in the South Pacific within 24 hours, according to diplomatic sources. This follows Australia's recen...

item.title: Canadian rescue team saves child amid Venezuela earthquake devastation
item.description: A Canadian urban search and rescue team from Burnaby played a critical role in locating a young survivor trapped in earthquake rubble in Venezuela, where over 3,342 people have died and 17,345 are hom...

item.title: Olivia Dean's Multiracial Heritage and Cultural Influences
item.description: Olivia Dean, a British singer of mixed ethnicity, has a unique cultural identity shaped by her English father and Jamaican-Guyanese mother. Born in London, she grew up in Highams Park, influenced by b...

item.title: Japan Funds New School Infrastructure in Tanzania
item.description: The Japanese government has invested over 269.3 million Tanzanian shillings to upgrade Orng’adida Primary School in Babati District, Manyara Region. The project, funded through Japan’s KUSANONE progra...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Election 2026: Karur Stampede Still Haunts Voters in Velusamypuram
item.description: The article discusses the lingering impact of a stampede incident that occurred during a political campaign in Tamil Nadu's Velusamypuram region. The incident, which happened during an election event,...

item.title: Gauteng Government Provides Trauma Counseling to Families of Ratanda Protest Victims
item.description: The Gauteng government has initiated trauma counseling for the families of two men killed during violent service delivery protests in Ratanda, following prolonged water outages that sparked community ...

item.title: House Prosecution Panel to Address Sara Duterte's Alleged Threats Against Marcos
item.description: The House prosecution panel has announced it will present evidence regarding Vice President Sara Duterte's alleged threats against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and his family, emphasizing that 'impu...

item.title: Switzerland's Energy Transition Doesn't Require New Nuclear Plants
item.description: A report by 19 energy specialists from ETH Zurich and the Paul Scherrer Institute concludes that Switzerland can achieve a secure energy transition through hydropower, solar energy, wind power, and el...

item.title: Tanzania Advances Nuclear Energy Plans with IAEA Support
item.description: Tanzania is making significant strides in its preparations to adopt nuclear energy for electricity generation, following the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) roadmap. The country is required ...

item.title: Security Forces Repel Gunmen Attack at NIPSS, Kill One Suspect
item.description: Security forces at the National Institute for Petroleum Technology and Exploration (NIPSS) successfully repelled a coordinated attack by armed assailants on Thursday. The incident, which occurred at t...

item.title: VAR decisions and refereeing changes reshape World Cup 2026 standards as England faces new challenges
item.description: The article discusses the evolving role of referees and VAR officials in international football, highlighting how their approach has shifted from the group stages to the knockout rounds. During a rece...

item.title: Neymar announces retirement from Brazil after World Cup exit
item.description: Brazilian football legend Neymar confirmed his retirement from international football following Brazil's early exit from the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The 34-year-old forward, who scored Brazil's only goal...

item.title: Lahore Police Identify Three Suspects in Foreign Women Sexual Assault Case via DNA Evidence
item.description: Lahore police have confirmed that the DNA of three suspects accused in the alleged kidnapping and gang rape of foreign women in Lahore’s Defence area matches forensic evidence. The investigation, whic...

item.title: WA by-election to test One Nation's challenge against Andrew Hastie
item.description: A by-election in a Perth suburb has emerged as a critical test for One Nation's political strategy following the resignation of a senior minister from the Cook government. The contest, set in a seat L...

item.title: Rare Color Photos Reveal Everyday Life in Tver Oblast, Russia, 1910
item.description: This article showcases a collection of 50 rare and beautiful color photos capturing everyday life in Tver Oblast, Russia, during 1910. Taken using the early Autochrome process, these images provide a ...

item.title: Supreme Court to hear plea over allegations of witness influence in Karur stampede case involving TVK ministers
item.description: The Supreme Court of India is scheduled to hear a petition concerning the Karur stampede case, in which allegations have been made that certain accused individuals, now serving as ministers in the TVK...

item.title: Blue Bombers edge Tiger-Cats in thrilling 14-13 overtime showdown
item.description: In a tightly contested matchup, the Winnipeg Blue Bombers secured a narrow 14-13 victory over the Hamilton Tiger-Cats in a dramatic CFL game. The decisive moment came in the final minutes when Blue Bo...

item.title: Analysis Reveals $3.8 Billion Loss for Trump Memecoin Investors
item.description: A cryptocurrency analytics firm, Nansen, has revealed that nearly 1 million individuals have suffered losses totaling $3.8 billion after investing in President Donald Trump's $TRUMP memecoin. The anal...

item.title: Justin Muturi Urges Opposition to Name Flagbearer, Backs Kalonzo Musyoka
item.description: Former Attorney General Justin Muturi has challenged the United Opposition to officially name its presidential flagbearer ahead of the 2027 elections, accusing President William Ruto of undermining th...

item.title: Legal strategies shaping African political landscapes
item.description: The article explores how African leaders are leveraging legal systems to suppress political opposition and consolidate power. It highlights the use of courts as tools for silencing dissent, with examp...
